LAW AND MOTION, 302, DEFENDANT SERVICE EMPLOYEES INTERNATIONAL UNION'S DEMURRER TO PORTIONS OF COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED. THE DEMURRER IS OVERRULED TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION BECAUSE "IT IS REASONABLE THAT AN INVESTIGATION OF THE ALLEGATIONS IN THE ORIGINAL DFEH COMPLAINT WOULD LEAD TO THE INVESTIGATION OF SUBSEQUENT DISCRIMINATORY ACTS," INCLUDING DISCRIMINATION BASED ON THE FACT PLAINTIFF'S DISABILITY REQUIRED HIM TO USE MEDICAL LEAVE. (BAKER V. CHILDREN'S HOSPITAL MEDICAL CENTER (1989) 209 CAL.APP.3D 1057, 1065.) THE DEMURRER IS OVERRULED AS TO THE SECOND AND THIRD CAUSES OF ACTION BECAUSE "[A] DEMURRER DOES NOT LIE TO A PORTION OF A CAUSE OF ACTION." (PH II, INC. V. SUP. CT. (1995) 33 CAL.APP.4TH 1680, 1683.). PREVAILING PARTY SHALL PREPARE FORM OF ORDER THAT COMPLIES WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK FELICIA GREEN; COURT REPORTER MELANIE GHENO, CSR NO. 7489 (302/EHG)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike
Off Calendar Jul-08-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Dow W. Patten, of Smith Patten, Tel. 415.402.0084, for plaintiffs;
Kerianne Steele, of Weinberg Roger & Rosenfeld, Tel. 510.337.1001, for defendants.

Ruling: A R G U E D. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT SERVICE EMPLOYEES INTERNATIONAL UNION'S Motion To Strike is granted. The words "The Family and Medical Leave Act (42 U.S.C. sec. 12101 et seq." are stricken from the caption page of the complaint. Plaintiff is not required to file an amended complaint to remove this language. Rather, this Order establishes that the reference to the FMLA is stricken. Order signed in open court. Judge Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk Felicia Green; Court Reporter Melanie Gheno, CSR No. 7489; Dept. 302. (302/EHG)

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-08-2015 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Dow W. Patten, of Smith Patten, Tel. 415.402.0084, for plaintiffs;
Kerianne Steele, of Weinberg Roger & Rosenfeld, Tel. 510.337.1001, for defendants.

Ruling: A R G U E D. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT SERVICE EMPLOYEES INTERNATIONAL UNION'S DEMURRER TO PORTIONS OF COMPLAINT is overruled. The demurrer is overruled to the first cause of action because "it is reasonable that an investigation of the allegations in the original DFEH complaint would lead to the investigation of subsequent discriminatory acts," including discrimination based on the fact Plaintiff's disability required him to use medical leave. (Baker v. Children's Hospital Medical Center (1989) 209 Cal.App.3d 1057, 1065.) The demurrer is overruled as to the second and third causes of action because "[a] demurrer does not lie to a portion of a cause of action." (PH II, Inc. v. Sup. Ct. (1995) 33 Cal.App.4th 1680, 1683.). Prevailing party shall prepare form of order that complies with CRC 3.1312. Judge Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk Felicia Green; Court Reporter Melanie Gheno, CSR No. 7489; Dept. 302. (302/EHG)
